functionalities: - :App: manages :GameState: and renders exactly one of four screens by the screen field: the start screen, the battle screen, the result screen, or the final screen. Transitions happen through state only, no URL routing.

- :App: uses a dark-fantasy plus IDE aesthetic: a near-black violet background, an engraved serif display font for titles, a monospace font for all code and console text, an ember-red and soul-purple accent palette with soft glows, and a layout responsive for desktop and mobile.

- The start screen shows the title "Demon Debugger" and the subtitle "Exorcise the bugs that haunt your code". Under the heading "Choose your weapon" it presents the four :Weapon: cards from :DemonData:, each showing its language, weaponName, weaponType, and flavor. The player selects one weapon; an "Enter the Dungeon" button is disabled until a weapon is chosen. Pressing it initializes :GameState: with the chosen language, hearts 3, score 0, combo 0, index 0, and screen "battle".

- The application reads all demons and weapons from :DemonData:. The current roster is the demons whose language matches the chosen :Language:, in file order. :DemonData: content is preserved exactly and never regenerated.

- The battle screen shows a status bar (three hearts reflecting remaining hearts, the current score, the chosen weaponName, and the combo count when 2 or more), a horizontal dungeon path of nodes representing the roster with a marker on the current demon, and the :Arena: with the :Hero: on the left and the current :Demon: (its spriteId image, name, title, and bugCategory tag) on the right.

- The battle interaction is a simulation. It shows the demon's codeSnippet in a console panel with a "Run the Code" button. Pressing it shows a brief "executing ritual..." line, then the demon's simulationFailOutput. The demon's options then appear as selectable patch choices with an "Apply Patch & Strike" button enabled once a patch is selected.

- When the player applies a patch, :App: compares the selected option id to the current demon's correctOptionId to determine whether the answer is correct, sets lastAnswerCorrect to that result, then resolves the outcome and shows the result screen.
- When the applied patch is correct, :App: plays the correct-answer :CombatAnimation:.
- When the applied patch is correct, :App: increments combo by one.
- When the applied patch is correct, :App: adds the current demon's points to score. When combo is 3 or more, it adds an additional bonus equal to half the demon's points.
- When the applied patch is correct, :App: also increments demonsDefeated by one and updates bestCombo to the larger of its current value and the current combo.
- When the applied patch is incorrect, :App: plays the wrong-answer :CombatAnimation:.
- When the applied patch is incorrect, :App: subtracts one heart and resets combo to 0.

- The result screen displays "Demon Exorcised" with a victory glow when lastAnswerCorrect is true, or "The bug grows stronger" when it is false. It also shows the current demon's lore in an italic highlighted line, its explanation, and the text of the correct fix.

- The result screen has a "Continue" button. Pressing it sends :App: to the final screen when hearts have reached 0 or no demons remain in the roster; otherwise, when lastAnswerCorrect is true it advances to the next demon and shows the battle screen, and when lastAnswerCorrect is false it returns to the same demon and shows the battle screen.
- :App: includes a helper function playSound that takes an event name, attempts to play the matching audio file from the public sounds folder, and silently does nothing if the file cannot be played.

- The final screen shows the final score, demons exorcised, and best combo, plus a :Rank: derived from score. It has a "Download Scroll of Conquest" button that downloads a JSON file containing the weapon, language, score, demons exorcised, best combo, rank, and an ISO timestamp. A "Descend Again" button resets :GameState: and returns to the start screen.

- :App: includes a serverless endpoint at /api/summon. The endpoint reads a Gemini API key from a server-side environment variable, and this key is read only on the server and never sent to the browser.

- The /api/summon endpoint asks Google Gemini to produce one :Demon: as JSON matching the :Demon: shape, validates that the returned object contains all required :Demon: fields, and responds with the demon on success or an error status when generation or validation fails.

- The start screen has a "Summon a Random Demon" button that triggers :AiSummon:. :AiSummon: requests a demon from /api/summon; on a successful valid response it uses that demon for the next battle, and on any failure, timeout, or invalid response it instead picks a random demon from :DemonData: so the game always continues.

- :App: must build into a static production bundle plus the serverless function, suitable for deployment on Vercel. The core game (everything except live AI summoning) must work with no environment variables configured.
